items.title: 'We have won': Trump signals end to Iran hostilities but rules out truce
items.summary: TEHRAN: President Donald Trump said on Friday that the United States was considering "winding down" military operations against Iran but ruled out a ceasefire with the Islamic republic. The US president said hostilities could soon end as objectives are close to being met, but he rejected a truce while "literally obliterating the other side". Trump added that other nations will have to take responsibility for policing the Strait of Hormuz shipping lane, though he said the United States would assist if asked.

items.title: Malaysian artist Layla Sania hits all the right festive notes [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian artist Layla Sania Khairuzikri is no stranger to producing festive songs. This year, after releasing her first Chinese New Year song, 'Xin Nian Re Nao' (A Lively Chinese New Year), listeners were given a sneak peek of her next project at the end of the music video. As Ramadan approached, Layla released the second song from the project, 'Raya Berseri'.

items.title: Despite the distance, Bangladeshi workers find the comfort of ‘home’ in KL for Hari Raya Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Celebrating Hari Raya away from home is never easy, but when you are thousands of kilometres away on a mission to provide for your loved ones, it's essential to recreate a sense of “home” to keep you going. “I came here when I was just 18 years old, and I remember that I used to cry, especially during the first three years of celebrating Hari Raya here because I missed my family,” said Salemusa, who works at a restaurant in KL. Salemusa said his colleagues would often comfort him during those early years.

items.title: UK grants US access to military bases for strikes on Iran
items.summary: LONDON: The British government has authorised the United States to use UK military bases for strikes against Iranian missile sites currently targeting shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Starmer had previously rejected US requests to use British bases, stating he needed to be satisfied that any military action was legal. However, he modified his stance after Iran conducted strikes on British allies across the Middle East.

items.title: Couple, 5-year-old son killed in Pulau Tioman motorcycle crash
items.summary: Rompin police chief Superintendent Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said the victims were Mohd Sabri Ja'afar, 42, a customer service officer at the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia, his wife Siti Aisyah Suhaime, 32, and their 5-year-old son, Muhammad Yusuf Abdurrahman. Preliminary investigations revealed that the victims were riding a Yamaha Ego Avantiz motorcycle from the Kampung Juara waterfall to a resort when the incident occurred about 4pm. Sharif Shai said further investigations found none of the victims were wearing helmets at the time of the crash.

items.title: TNB's 'Raya Pertama' film highlights unity, strengthens bonds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) has released its latest Hari Raya festive film, "Raya Pertama", which underscores the importance of unity, empathy and community spirit in an increasingly fast-paced modern society. TNB group corporate communications chief Samsul Ariffin Zainuddin said the story reflects the growing disconnect in community values amid urban lifestyles and encourages Malaysians to return to the fundamentals of building harmonious relationships. "This reflects TNB's commitment to illuminating the lives of Malaysians.

items.title: Son of Astro Awani editor killed in crash with RTD vehicle
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The son of an Astro Awani senior editor died in a road crash involving a Road Transport Department (RTD) patrol vehicle at Km85.5 Jalan Kota Baru-Machang-Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, Bukit Bunga, in Tanah Merah this evening. The victim, identified as Mursyid Azmi, 25, son of Husna Yusop, was driving a Perodua Myvi when the collision occurred. Meanwhile, RTD senior enforcement director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan said the accident occurred about 4.30pm when the victim's vehicle was believed to have skidded into the opposite lane and collided with a Toyota Fortuner driven by an RTD officer.

items.title: Body found floating in Sungai Buloh lake, police seek public's help
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH: Police recovered the body of an unidentified man, believed to be a foreigner, from Tasik Biru in Kuang yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said police received information from the public about the incident at 8.03am and rushed to the scene. He urged anyone with information or who may have a missing family member matching the description to contact investigating officer Sergeant Major Rosli Mohd Nor at 013-6095744 or to contact the nearest police station.

items.title: Action movie star Chuck Norris dies
items.summary: Los Angeles, United States: Chuck Norris, the US martial artist and Hollywood action star most famous for his role in "Walker, Texas Ranger," has died, his family said Friday. "It is with heavy hearts that our family shares the sudden passing of our beloved Chuck Norris yesterday morning," the family said in a statement on Instagram. Four years later, his epic fight with the kung-fu superstar Bruce Lee in "The Way of The Dragon" helped turn Norris into an icon on big and small screen alike.

items.title: Anwar welcomes temporary Pakistan-Afghanistan ceasefire, hopes it will continue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed a temporary ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan during Aidilfitri. He also reaffirmed Malaysia's commitment to strengthening ties with Pakistan and called for continued de-escalation efforts in conflict-hit regions. He said he and Shehbaz Sharif exchanged Aidilfitri greetings and discussed bilateral cooperation.

items.title: Hamzah calls for 'reset' as global tensions threaten economy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must be prepared to navigate growing global uncertainties amid escalating geopolitical tensions that could impact the economy and cost of living, says opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. In his Hari Raya Aidilfitri message, he stressed the need for proactive measures and clear national direction to safeguard the rakyat against potential economic shocks. Hamzah said ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, including developments involving Israel, the United States and Iran, risk triggering wider economic repercussions globally.

items.title: Langkawi ferry schedule to be restructured over rising diesel costs
items.summary: ALOR STAR: The ferry service schedule to Langkawi will be revised after the Aidilfitri holidays due to rising industrial diesel prices by over 100 per cent. Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd general manager Dr Baharin Baharom said the restructuring is needed to reduce the risk of losses faced by Langkawi ferry operators as operational costs increase. "At present, ferry operators in Langkawi charge a fare of 88 sen per nautical mile, which is significantly lower than the rates charged by operators for other major islands, such as Pulau Pangkor and Pulau Tioman, which reach RM2 per nautical mile.

items.title: Anwar calls for moderation, compassion in Aidilfitri celebrations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says the people should celebrate Aidilfitri in moderation and be more mindful of the plight of those who are suffering, as the world faces geopolitical turmoil, especially in the Middle East. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said that with proper preparation and planning, the country remains optimistic about the economic position it has achieved, especially in recent years. "And we, the Madani government, will strive to defend and strengthen this position as challenges mount," he said.

items.title: News@9: Today's top headlines - March 20, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: Safeguard unity Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim urged Malaysians to preserve harmony and resolve disputes quickly to prevent them from escalating into chaos. No panic buying Agriculture Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu assured that food supplies remain sufficient and urged Malaysians not to panic buy amid global uncertainties. iOS security alert The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has urged Apple users to update their devices immediately to fix critical "DarkSword" vulnerabilities that could expose sensitive data.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Friday, March 20, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1401169]] [[nid:1401168]] [[nid:1401167]] [[nid:1401166]] [[nid:1401165]] [[nid:1401164]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MCBA, RTD team up to stop fuel smuggling at Rantau Panjang ICQS
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) will conduct joint operations with the Road Transport Department to curb petrol and diesel being smuggled into Thailand. Rantau Panjang MCBA deputy commander Bakri Sulaiman said the agency had detected that both lorries and sedan cars were being used by smugglers to transport fuel to the neighbouring country via the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complex. He said the smugglers were using legal routes to send Malaysian subsidised fuel to Thailand following the fuel shortage there.

items.title: Traffic in Gua Musang expected to surge tonight ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Police are expecting traffic volume in Gua Musang to increase until tonight due to the influx of people returning home for Aidilfitri, said Gua Musang district police chief, Superintendent Sik Choon Foo. He said vehicles from the Klang Valley are still entering the district in large numbers, while several vehicles had broken down along Jalan Gua Musang-Kuala Lipis, Pahang. "Traffic congestion at the border area of Merapoh, Pahang and Kampung Mentara here exceeded 20km due to several broken-down vehicles along the road.

items.title: DPMs Zahid, Fadillah highlight kinship, unity and prudence in Aidilfitri messages (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on Muslims to continue fostering love, strengthening bonds of kinship and extending sincere forgiveness to one another in conjunction with Syawal. He also prayed that the Syawal celebrations would bring blessings, peace and happiness to all Muslims. https://www.facebook.com/zahidhamidi.fanpage/videos/saya-ingin-mengucapkan-selamat-hari-raya-aidilfitri-maaf-zahir-dan-batin-kepada-/2303819680028279/ Meanwhile, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof urged Muslims to celebrate Aidilfitri this year in a modest yet meaningful way.

items.title: Factory fire in Korea leaves 55 injured, more than a dozen unaccounted for [WATCH]
items.summary: SEOUL: A fire broke out around lunchtime at a car parts factory in the South Korean city of Daejeon on Friday, injuring 55 people, including 24 seriously, while 14 others were unaccounted for, fire authorities said. Phone data showed their locations near where the fire broke out, but officials said search efforts were hampered after the building partially collapsed in the blaze. Many employees were believed to have been in a second-floor break room when the fire started, fire authorities said.

items.title: Belgian bishop challenges Pope Leo to allow married priests by 2028
items.summary: BRUSSELS/VATICAN CITY: A Catholic bishop said he would push the Vatican for permission to ordain married men as priests by 2028, in an unusual statement that may test Pope Leo's willingness to change Church teaching to address the issue of dwindling clergy numbers. Johan Bonny, bishop of Antwerp in Belgium since 2009 and known for taking progressive positions, said in a public letter to his parishioners released on Thursday that he would make "every effort" to ordain married men within two years and would identify men to train as priests. If Bonny went ahead and ordained married priests without the Vatican's permission, he could be subject to excommunication, a formal expulsion from the Church.

items.title: Two killed, four injured after car skids and hits tree in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 20 — Two men were killed while four others were seriously injured in an accident that occurred in Precinct 1 heading towards Taman Wetland, Precinct 12, here this afternoon. Putrajaya Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director PgB M Haziq Hazmi said his department received an emergency call via NG999 at 11.58 am before a fire engine from Precinct 14 Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) with an EMRS team rushed to the scene. “A total of six victims were involved in the incident with two men confirmed dead at the scene by the Ministry of Health,” he said via a Facebook post by the Federal Territory of Putrajaya Fire and Rescue Service today.

items.title: State leaders call for stronger kinship and spirit of togetherness this Aidilfitri (VIDEO)
items.summary: Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the festive day offers all Muslims a chance to deepen relationships, seek forgiveness, and show appreciation for friends, families and the surrounding community. May this Aidilfitri bring happiness, peace, and blessings to all,” he said in his Aidilfitri greeting in a Facebook post today. https://www.facebook.com/dsaminuddinharun/videos/selamat-hari-raya-aidilfitri-maaf-zahir-dan-batin-kepada-seluruh-rakyat-negeri-s/1275481581346349/ Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail called on the people to make Aidilfitri a moment to strengthen family and community ties and to rekindle the spirit of love among the people.

items.title: Lights, drawings and handmade touches: Anwar ‘truly touched’, thanks students for creative Aidilfitri cards (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed his appreciation to students and Malaysians for the creative Hari Raya Aidilfitri greeting cards sent to him in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri tomorrow. “Alhamdulillah, I am truly touched to receive various colourful Hari Raya cards showcasing the creativity of children, some with drawings, some even with lights, truly creative! Salam Aidilfitri to everyone,” he said.

items.title: ‘Strengthen unity this Raya’: Ministers urge Malaysians to uphold unity, kindness and mutual respect in Aidilfitri greetings (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Cabinet ministers urged Malaysians to use Aidilfitri celebrations as ways to strengthen unity, encourage kindness and solidarity, as well as enhance humanitarian values in the country’s multicultural society. In the spirit of unity, let us continue to cultivate respect, kindness and concern for each other,” he said. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R Ramanan meanwhile invited Malaysians to continue to strengthen their unity, instill kindness and to step forward in the spirit of togetherness towards a brighter future.

items.title: Lorry overturns, causing oil spill on Pan Borneo Highway near Bukit Song; no injuries reported
items.summary: MIRI, March 20 — No injuries were reported following a road incident involving an oil spill after a cargo lorry overturned while descending Bukit Song along the Pan Borneo Highway here on Thursday evening. The Fire and Rescue Department Malaysia (Bomba) Lopeng said it received a distress call at 5.37pm and dispatched a team from the Lopeng fire station to the scene, located about 30km away. Upon arrival at 6.07pm, the operation commander PBK I Rorudi Umas reported that the oil spill was believed to have been caused by a self-accident involving a cargo lorry.

items.title: As global fuel prices climb, how does Malaysia compare with other countries?
items.summary: RON95 now costs RM3.27 per litre and RON97 is RM4.55 per litre, up from RM2.59 and RM3.15 per litre respectively at the end of February. Diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has climbed from RM3.04 to RM4.72 per litre over the same period, while diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan remains at RM2.15 per litre. Regular fuel in the US is priced at RM4.05 (US$1.02) per litre, while the UK’s E10 petrol stands at RM7.66 (1.45 pounds) per litre.

items.title: Zahid Hamidi calls on Muslims to strengthen bonds of kinship this Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on Muslims to continue fostering love, strengthening bonds of kinship and extending sincere forgiveness to one another in conjunction with Syawal. In a Facebook post, he reminded the people to cherish the moments spent with family and loved ones during the Aidilfitri celebrations. He also prayed that the Syawal celebrations would bring blessings, peace and happiness to all Muslims.

items.title: Husband finds wife dead in pool of blood at home
items.summary: Police reportedly seized a knife. Authorities found no signs of criminal activity. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Resolve minor disputes early to protect unity, stability and national security, says PM Anwar
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Any minor disputes in the country must be addressed promptly to prevent them from escalating into crises that could undermine peace, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He stressed that unity among the people, as well as national stability and security, must remain top priorities as Malaysia faces economic pressures and uncertainties stemming from international conflicts. “Safeguard national peace, especially as we are a country of diverse races and religions.

items.title: Bargain hunters pack Jalan TAR bazaar as traders clear stock
items.summary: They also sought the best bargains as traders reduced prices further to clear stock on the final day of the fasting month. Trader Juliana Sharin, 39, who operates a stall selling Indonesian-inspired baju kebaya, said her stall would remain open until 4am. Juliana said this marked her second year at the bazaar.

items.title: Firefighters battle massive bushfire in Kudat
items.summary: KUDAT: Firefighters took more than three hours to bring a large bushfire here under control. The bushfire occurred in Kampung Naradang Darat Sikuati, about 20km from the nearest fire station. Firefighters deployed fire beaters and portable backpack pumps to contain the flames, working under challenging field conditions.

items.title: Smooth traffic on major highways as last-minute Raya travellers head home
items.summary: A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said that closed-circuit television (CCTV) monitoring indicated steady vehicle movement on northbound, southbound and East Coast-bound routes. "Slight congestion was only detected in a small section near the Seremban Rest and Service (R&R) area heading towards Bandar Ainsdale; however, this has not impacted the overall traffic flow," he said today. In view of the clear roads, LLM issued a stern warning to motorists against speeding or taking advantage of the light traffic to violate traffic laws, citing an increased risk of road accidents.

items.title: Raya rush eases: Traffic smooth on major highways this evening with only slight congestion near Seremban R&R
items.summary: A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said that closed-circuit television (CCTV) monitoring indicated steady vehicle movement on northbound, southbound and East Coast-bound routes. “Slight congestion was only detected in a small section near the Seremban Rest and Service (R&R) area heading towards Bandar Ainsdale; however, this has not impacted the overall traffic flow,” he said when contacted by Bernama today. In view of the clear roads, LLM issued a stern warning to motorists against speeding or taking advantage of the light traffic to violate traffic laws, citing an increased risk of road accidents.

items.title: Firefighters rescue man found unconscious in locked car
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A 37-year-old man was rescued after being found unconscious inside a locked car near Kepayan this afternoon. The Fire and Rescue Department was alerted at 2.49pm, and 11 firefighters were despatched from Putatan. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: DPM Fadillah urges prudent electricity, water use during Aidilfitri
items.summary: Fadillah added that water supply operators are on alert to ensure demand is met efficiently. “The National Water Services Commission (SPAN) also ensures operators are prepared with contingency plans in case of any unexpected incidents during the festive season,” said Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation. The Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (PETRA), through SPAN, also targets a maximum daily per capita water usage of 160 litres, in line with international benchmarks.

items.title: Ministers urge M'sians to maintain harmony, unity in Aidilfitri greetings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Cabinet ministers urged Malaysians to use Aidilfitri celebrations as ways to strengthen unity, encourage kindness and solidarity, as well as enhance humanitarian values in the country's multicultural society. "Technology must be used to strengthen solidarity and spread kindness, not as dividers that seperate us. "As Malaysians living in a multicultural country, let's carry on instilling values of mutual respect, tolerance and solidarity.

items.title: Police expect heavier traffic in Gua Musang tonight as Raya travellers head home
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, March 20 — Police are expecting traffic in Gua Musang to rise till tonight due to the influx of people coming back to celebrate Aidilfitri, Gua Musang district police chief Supt Sik Choon Foo said. Vehicles from the Klang Valley are still entering the district in droves, while there are several vehicles that have broken down along Jalan Gua Musang-Kuala Lipis, Pahang, he added. Checks by Bernama as of 5 pm revealed that traffic volume has risen along Kampung Lepan Jaya near Kampung Meranto, while traffic is slow in front of Dewan Kompleks Perdana Majlis Daerah Gua Musang to the Bendahara traffic light junction and along the entire stretch of Jalan Gua Musang-Kuala Krai.

items.title: Anwar touched by children's Raya cards ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed appreciation to students and Malaysians who sent him Hari Raya Aidilfitri greeting cards, describing them as heartfelt gestures ahead of the celebration. "Alhamdulillah, I am truly touched to receive various colourful Hari Raya cards showcasing the creativity of children — some with drawings, some even with lights, truly creative," he said. In the four-minute video, Anwar is seen reading several cards, including those from Sekolah Kebangsaan Muadzam Jaya in Pahang and Orang Asli pupils from Sekolah Kebangsaan Talang in Negeri Sembilan.

items.title: State leaders: Aidilfitri platform to strengthen kinship, spirit of togetherness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: State leaders have expressed hope that tomorrow's Aidilfitri celebrations will serve as a meaningful occasion to strengthen ties of kinship, cultivate love and foster a spirit of togetherness in the community. May this Aidilfitri bring happiness, peace, and blessings to all," he said in his Aidilfitri greeting in a Facebook post today. Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail called on the people to make Aidilfitri a moment to strengthen family and community ties and to rekindle the spirit of love among the people.

items.title: China overtakes EU in car and parts trade for first time
items.summary: STUTTGART: The balance of power in the global car market is shifting, with imports of cars and automotive parts from China into the European Union surpassing EU exports to China for the first time, according to an analysis by consultancy EY, reported German news agency dpa. Exports of cars and parts from the EU to China fell by 34 per cent last year to €16 billion (US$18.5 billion), the report said. Since the peak in 2022, German exports to China have more than halved from around €30 billion to €13.6 billion, while vehicle imports from China rose by two-thirds to €7.4 billion.

items.title: ‘Celebrate, but be prudent’: Anwar urges moderation this Aidilfitri as global conflicts strain prices
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to celebrate Aidilfitri in a moderate and prudent manner, taking into account current economic challenges and global uncertainties. “Of course, we want to celebrate (Aidilfitri), but I would like to urge moderation. Muslims in Malaysia will celebrate Aidilfitri tomorrow.

items.title: Anwar urges moderate Raya celebrations amid global pressures
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to celebrate Aidilfitri in a moderate and prudent manner, citing current economic challenges and global uncertainties. Speaking at a Ramadan programme with Permatang Pauh community leaders at the Permatang Pasir Community Hall, Anwar pointed to ongoing global conflicts as a source of economic strain. So we must save a little," he said.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in six states until 7pm today, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds in Perak, Kelantan, Pahang, Selangor, Sabah and Sarawak until 7 pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement today that the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. Also affected are Jeli and Gua Musang in Kelantan; Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis and Raub), Hulu Selangor in Selangor, the interior of Sabah (Nabawan) and Tawau; as well as Betong, Sarikei, Sibu (Selangau) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro) in Sarawak.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Thunderstorms expected in six states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning for thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds in Perak, Kelantan, Pahang, Selangor, Sabah and Sarawak until 7pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement the affected areas in Perak are Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim. Also affected are Jeli and Gua Musang in Kelantan; Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis and Raub), Hulu Selangor in Selangor, the interior of Sabah (Nabawan) and Tawau; as well as Betong, Sarikei, Sibu (Selangau) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis and Daro) in Sarawak.

items.title: PJ police: Driver in viral video of Myvi ramming others on NKVE wanted under POCA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Police arrested a suspect wanted under the Prevention of Crime Act (POCA) last Wednesday following a pursuit along the New Klang Valley Expressway (NKVE). Petaling Jaya District Police Chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the suspect, in his 20s and driving a blue Perodua Myvi, attempted to evade a police inspection and drove aggressively, colliding with several vehicles before being stopped by a mobile patrol vehicle (MPV) on the Klang-bound route at 2.10 pm. “Checks revealed that the local man had seven prior criminal records and was also wanted for an offence under Section 15(4) of POCA in Kuala Bera, Pahang.

items.title: ‘This is not the time for division,’ Anwar says as Malaysia grapples with oil price shock
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Malaysia’s economy has remained stable despite a sharp global surge in oil prices, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today, assuring the public that the government is taking measures to cushion the impact on consumers. “Previously, prices were manageable. Now, with oil and gas installations being attacked and supply routes disrupted, prices have surged sharply,” Anwar said during a community gathering in Permatang Pauh.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia increasing border surveillance amid Middle East turmoil
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today that Malaysia will heighten its border surveillance in response to growing turmoil and conflict in the Middle East. The prime minister said that maintaining internal stability is crucial to weathering external threats, warning that even minor domestic disputes must be handled decisively to prevent escalation. “Even small quarrels must be addressed immediately.

items.title: PM observes last day of Ramadan with Penang community ahead of Aidilfitri tomorrow
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim began his one-day working visit to Penang today by performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jamek As-Syakirin in Kampung Pelet here. He arrived at the mosque at 1.12 pm and was received by Permatang Pauh UMNO division chief Datuk Mohd Zaidi Mohd Said, mosque chairman Othman Syafie and congregants. Anwar, together with nearly 700 worshippers, including Penang Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Dr Mohamad Abdul Hamid, then listened to a sermon titled “Ramadan, Ketika Senja Terakhir Berlabuh” delivered by Imam I Muhammad Nasir Hamid.

items.title: M40 yet to feel full benefits of economic growth, says BNM governor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Malaysia’s middle-income (M40) group has yet to fully benefit from economic growth, with many still facing cost of living pressures, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said. According to a report by Berita Harian , he said stagnant wages among the group require labour market reforms to create higher-value jobs with better pay. “The government’s main focus is to create high-value jobs that can offer better wages,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: JB Southkey murder: Five suspects arrested, four nabbed in Singapore with SPF’s help
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 20 — Five foreign men have been arrested for their alleged involvement in the murder of a man in the Southkey commercial area here last week. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said that following the murder, investigators from the state’s Serious Crimes Unit (D9) moved quickly to identify the suspects. Ab Rahaman said the first suspect apprehended in Johor has been remanded until March 24, while the four arrested in Singapore are remanded until March 26 to assist with the investigation.

items.title: Guan Eng mocks Penang CM over land tax, says 90pc revisions show system is broken
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Former Penang chief minister Lim Guan Eng has demanded accountability from the state government after land tax bills were revised downward by as much as 90 per cent following errors by the Land and Mines Office. “If the land tax rate increase calculation system was indeed correct, fair, orderly and professional, what need would there be to reduce land tax by 80 per cent or 90 per cent?” he said. Lim cited additional cases of extreme tax increases affecting ordinary residents, including a kampung house in Juru whose land tax rose 2,842 times from RM12 to RM34,118, and a 57-year-old man whose ancestral land tax surged 727 times from RM29 to RM21,103.

items.title: Motorcyclist assaulted with baseball bat in Kulai after ‘terawih’ prayers
items.summary: The incident, which occurred at 9.50pm, allegedly stemmed from a road altercation where the victim was accused of cutting into the assailants’ car. Kulai district police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee confirmed that the victim had lodged a report and that police were aware of a video of the incident circulating online that matches the report. “The car was then driven recklessly before it intercepted the victim, forcing him to stop,” Tan explained.

items.title: Three die after Proton X70 collides with Alza on Pan Borneo Highway
items.summary: KUCHING, March 20 — Three people were reportedly killed and five others injured in a crash involving two vehicles on the Pan Borneo Highway along the Serian–Sri Aman route near Kampung Selepong Jinggau today. “Two boys in the same vehicle sustained head injuries and were given initial treatment by the Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team before being sent to Sri Aman Hospital,” a JBPM spokesperson said. JBPM said the three victims who died were travelling in the Proton X70 — Lau Sie Tung, 70, and two women, Grace Lau Chi Ai, 22, and Lau Yok Leh, 22.

items.title: PLUS expects heavy traffic until night as festive travel peaks ahead of Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — PLUS Malaysia Berhad reportedly expects traffic on the country’s major highways to remain heavy until late tonight as people travel back to their hometowns for Aidilfitri. Its senior general manager of operations Mohd Yusuf Abdul Aziz said daily traffic still exceeded two million vehicles despite a slight drop from the previous day, according to a report in Berita Harian. He reportedly said that accidents have been handled quickly through cooperation between PLUS, the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and the Road Transport Department (JPJ).

items.title: Melaka food delivery rider remanded over violent attack on pregnant wife
items.summary: MELAKA, March 20 — A man who beat his pregnant wife on Sunday has been remanded for four days at the Ayer Keroh Magistrate’s Court here, starting today. Yesterday, the police arrested a man who was accused of beating his pregnant wife, in a house in Taman Seri Kemendor, Jasin. Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit was reported to have said that the 22-year-old suspect was arrested by a team of police officers from the D8 and D11 units, Criminal Investigation Department (CID), Melaka police contingent headquarters (IPK) at around 11 am.

items.title: In Aidilfitri greetings, MCCBCHST calls for national unity and social cohesion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 – The Malaysian Consultative Council of Buddhism, Christianity, Hinduism, Sikhism and Taoism (MCCBCHST) has extended its greetings to the Muslim community as the nation prepares to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri on March 21. In a statement released today, the interfaith council described the festival as a significant time for reflection, gratitude, and renewal. MCCBCHST also said that racial and religious harmony remains the bedrock of Malaysia’s peace, stability, and ongoing progress.

items.title: Heavy rain and strong winds alert for several districts in Sabah, Sarawak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until noon today. In a statement, MetMalaysia said that in Sabah, the areas expected to be affected are Tawau (Tawau and Semporna), while in Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching (Lundu and Kuching), Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Sarikei and Meradong) and Mukah (Tanjung Manis, Daro, Matu and Dalat). — Bernama

items.title: Scammers use fake KL traffic police letterhead to solicit Raya donations, public warned
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) has denied issuing an invitation letter for a Hari Raya Aidilfitri event that also asked for donations from the public. Kuala Lumpur JSPT chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa in a statement today said that his department detected the distribution of the fake letter via WhatsApp application at 5.50 pm yesterday. “Any confirmation can be made directly with the Kuala Lumpur JSPT at 03-2071 9999, the Kuala Lumpur JSPT Hotline at 03-2026 0267/ 0269 or any nearby police station,” he said.

items.title: Raya exodus: LLM says most routes under control except two key highways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Traffic flow on several major highways was slow this morning as people began returning to their hometowns to celebrate Aidilfitri tomorrow. Congestion has also been detected on the KL-Karak Expressway heading east, with slow-moving traffic stretching 9.8 kilometres (km) from KM48.1 to KM57.9 (Bukit Tinggi). However, traffic conditions at the location remain manageable.

items.title: Government backbenchers hail 50pc toll discount, Raya aid measures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — The MADANI Government Backbenchers Club (BBC) has welcomed and expressed full support for the government’s immediate measures to safeguard the well-being of the people ahead of the upcoming Hari Raya Aidilfitri. BBC chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa said the approach taken by the government reflects a structured and targeted intervention framework, not only in easing the cost of living but also in strengthening the momentum of the people’s economic recovery. She said the approach encompasses key aspects, including cost of living, social welfare and market stability.
